docs.text: Chrony Clients Setup with Ansible This guide explains how to configure Chrony on Linux clients using Ansible. This should be used to make sure that the camera and system times closely match between recording setups. It covers inventory setup, group variables, playbook usage, debugging, and troubleshooting tips. Overview Chrony is a versatile NTP client/server suite. In our lab, we standardize time synchronization using a central NTP server ( ntp_server_ip ). This guide ensures all clients sync correctly and consistently. The NTP server is currently installed in the synology storage NAS. Prerequisites Before running the playbook, ensure: Ansible 2.16+ is installed on the control node. Python 3.x is installed on target hosts. SSH access to all clients in the inventory. The inventory and group variables are defined correctly. Inventory & Group Variables tree roles/chrony_client/ roles/chrony_client/ ├── handlers │ └── main.yml ├── install_chrony.yml ├── tasks │ ├── configure.yml │ ├── install.yml │ ├── main.yml │ ├── metrics.yml │ └── sync.yml └── templates └── chrony.conf.j2 Inventory Example ```ini inventory/hosts.ini [aixlab] aixlab-01 aixlab-02 inventory/group_vars/ntp_clients.yml ntp_server_ip: "192.168.10.201" chrony_conf_path: /etc/chrony/chrony.conf Tip: Always reference variables using {{ ntp_server_ip }}, not the IP directly inside curly braces. chrony_clients.yml: name: Configure Chrony client hosts: aixlab become: yes roles: chrony_client example task: - name: Ensure Chrony configuration points to the correct NTP server ansible.builtin.lineinfile: path: "{{ chrony_conf_path }}" regexp: "^server" line: "server {{ ntp_server_ip }} iburst" state: present name: Restart chrony service ansible.builtin.service: name: chronyd state: restarted debugging: - name: Debug ntp_server_ip before using it ansible.builtin.debug: var: ntp_server_ip
docs.title: Time Sync Between Computers

docs.text: This guide explains how to configure Chrony on Linux clients using Ansible. This should be used to make sure that the camera and system times closely match between recording setups. It covers inventory setup, group variables, playbook usage, debugging, and troubleshooting tips.
